2 min read
1 hour ago

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I’ve been putting off writing this for like two weeks now. Kept telling myself I’d do it “this weekend.” Anyway, here it is.
I’m Hasnain Alam I build projects with the MERN stack mostly, MongoDB, Express, React js, Node js, that whole combo. Somewhere around 2 years ago I got pulled into React Native because a client needed an Android app and I didn’t want to say no, and then Electron happened for pretty much the same reason someone needed a desktop tool and “just use the browser” wasn’t gonna cut it.
Honestly I didn’t plan any of this out. I didn’t sit down one day and decide “I’ll be a full-stack dev who also does mobile and desktop.” It just kind of happened project by project. You get thrown into something, you figure it out, and then that thing becomes part of what you do. React Native specifically I remember the first app I built with it took me almost three times longer than it should have because I kept trying to think in web-dev terms instead of actually learning how RN handles things differently.
What I actually enjoy is the debugging part, weirdly. Not writing new code fixing broken code. There’s something satisfying about a bug that makes zero sense until suddenly it does.
What I Actually Work On
A rough breakdown of where I spend my time:
*Web apps*: full-stack builds using the MERN stack, from database schema to the last pixel of UI
*Mobile apps*: cross-platform apps with React Native, trying to keep them feeling native, not like a website wearing a mobile costume
*Desktop apps*: Electron.js builds for people who want their tool to live outside the browser
*The unglamorous stuff*: performance optimization, cleaning up other people’s (and my own) technical debt, and writing code that the next developer won’t curse me for
Why I’m Here on Medium
I’m starting this blog for two reasons. First, writing things down forces me to actually understand them if I can’t explain a concept clearly, I probably don’t know it as well as I think I do. Second, I’ve learned a lot from other developers’ write-ups over the years, and it feels right to start putting something back into that pool.
Expect posts on practical, real-world development things I’ve actually built, bugs I’ve actually hit, and the kind of solutions that don’t always make it into the official documentation. No fluff, no “10 JavaScript Tricks You Didn’t Know” listicles — just honest notes from someone building things day to day.
Let’s Connect
If you want to see what I’ve been building or just want to say hi:
Portfolio: hasnainalam.com
GitHub: github.com/devhasnain
More posts coming soon. Thanks for reading the first one.
